payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

original_descriptions: Weekly pocket money task completion and tracking cycle
Enable children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks
Provide children visibility into task inventory availability and completion status

payload.name: Household Participant Roles
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for defining and recognizing the distinct participant roles within the household ecosystem, establishing who has standing to interact with the enterprise and in what capacity.

payload.name: Compensated Work Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to governing the lifecycle of compensated work arrangements, including the definition of work opportunities, their monetary valuation, and the accountability record of work performed and claimed.

payload.name: Financial Obligation Transparency
payload.description: Enterprise responsibility for providing clear, timely visibility into earned compensation and ensuring stakeholders can understand and verify financial obligations owed within the defined compensation cycle.

payload.name: Performance Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise obligation to maintain authoritative records of participant performance, enabling oversight, verification of compensation distribution accuracy, and longitudinal analysis of contribution patterns.

payload.name: Task Specification Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing and governing the tasks offered within the household, including the monetary values attached to each task and their recurring availability within defined cycles.

payload.name: Completion Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for children to claim accountability for completed work, establishing the relationship between child effort and entitlement to reward recognition.

payload.name: Earnings Settlement Record
payload.description: Business responsibility for accumulating and presenting the monetary entitlement earned by children within defined periods, supporting the fulfillment of payment obligations by parents.

payload.name: Historical Activity Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for preserving task and earnings records across time periods to enable auditability, trend analysis, and sustained accountability beyond current operational cycles.

payload.name: Task Lifecycle Model
payload.description: Logical representation of task state transitions, availability cycles, and historical persistence requirements governing how tasks move through their operational lifecycle from creation through completion and archival.

payload.name: Child Data Visibility Model
payload.description: Logical design of access boundaries and visibility constraints determining which task and earnings information child actors may view or interact with based on relational context and role-based scoping rules.

payload.name: Platform Version Compatibility
payload.description: Physical constraints governing operating system version requirements and compatibility boundaries for deployment infrastructure and runtime environments.

payload.name: Multi-Platform Deployment Configuration
payload.description: Detailed specifications for deploying application components across iOS, Android, and Windows desktop platforms, including platform-specific runtime configurations, build parameters, and deployment topology details.

payload.name: Child User Interface Specification
payload.description: Detailed interface specifications for child actor interactions, including precise UI component definitions for task claiming, viewing available tasks, completed task displays, and weekly earnings access timing parameters.

payload.name: Parent User Interface Specification
payload.description: Detailed interface specifications for parent actor interactions, including precise UI component definitions for task configuration controls and distribution report access screens.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognize child participants as distinct actors with standing to interact within the household ecosystem.
payload.rationale: Establishes enterprise accountability for acknowledging child users as a discrete participant role within the household context, ensuring their capacity to engage is formally recognized at the scope level.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognize parent participants as distinct actors with standing to interact within the household ecosystem.
payload.rationale: Establishes enterprise accountability for acknowledging parent users as a discrete participant role within the household context, ensuring their capacity to engage is formally recognized at the scope level.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall differentiate between child and parent participant roles within the household ecosystem.
payload.rationale: Captures the enterprise commitment to maintain role distinction as a fundamental aspect of household participant governance, deriving from the existence of multiple distinct actor types.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable authorized participants to establish compensated work opportunities with defined monetary values.
payload.rationale: Derives from the enterprise goal to permit parents to define task assignments with compensation, requiring both the capability to establish work opportunities and to assign monetary value to them.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility into the inventory of available and completed compensated work opportunities.
payload.rationale: Addresses the enterprise commitment to transparency regarding task availability and completion status for authorized participants.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain an authoritative record of compensated work performed and claimed.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise obligation to retain an accountable record of task completion events and claims, forming the basis for compensation stewardship.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall support the claiming of completed compensated work by authorized participants.
payload.rationale: Derived from the goal to enable children to claim completed tasks, representing the enterprise commitment to providing a mechanism for work completion acknowledgment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall operate on a weekly cycle for task completion, tracking, and earnings summary reporting.
payload.rationale: Establishes the temporal cadence governing the financial obligation transparency cycle at enterprise scope

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility of weekly earnings summary to children.
payload.rationale: Core obligation for financial transparency, unifying the goal of enabling viewing with the capability to calculate and display earnings

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain authoritative records of child performance sufficient to support compensation distribution verification.
payload.rationale: Parent stakeholders require access to child performance data to verify that compensation distribution is accurate and aligned with actual contribution. The enterprise commits to maintaining these records as an accountability obligation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide stakeholders access to longitudinal task history for accountability and trend analysis purposes.
payload.rationale: Stakeholders need temporal visibility into participant contribution patterns to fulfill oversight obligations and identify performance trends over time. This is a commitment to transparency and informed governance.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Requirement elements present in the ledger.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.
